slides.title: Celebrations and Festivals
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 7 English, Unit 2: Celebrations and Festivals. Welcome to Unit 2! In this unit you will explore celebrations and festivals from around the world, with a special focus on traditions that matter to communities across East Africa and the Muslim world. Festivals bring people together. They remind us of our history, our values, and the things that make each culture unique. Whether it is the joy of Eid al-Fitr after a month of fasting, the colourful lanterns of a harvest celebration, or the excitement of a local sports day, every festival has a story to tell. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and understand texts about different festivals and celebrations
Use prepositions of time (in, on, at) correctly in speech and writing
Recognise and form compound adjectives (hand-made, brightly-coloured)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and understand texts about different festivals and celebrations. you will also use prepositions of time (in, on, at) correctly in speech and writing. you will also recognise and form compound adjectives (hand-made, brightly-coloured). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: benevolent, charismatic, determined, diligent, enthusiastic, industrious. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Celebrations and Festivals: source text 1 and Celebrations and Festivals: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: How to Use Them: Step by Step
bullets: Step 1 — Use IN for longer periods of time: Think of in as a big container that holds a stretch of time.
slides.narration: The first language focus is How to Use Them: Step by Step. Step 1 — Use IN for longer periods of time: Think of in as a big container that holds a stretch of time. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Compare Two Festivals. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
